item.title: 00 Applying to Join
item.description: Summary This page explains how to join the CAPIBARA Collaboration as a student at any level.

item.title: 11 Unix
item.description: ABSTRACT The goal of this coding tutorial is to set up a unix-like environment on your computer.

item.title: 13 Git
item.description: ABSTRACT Git is essentially a “Time Machine” for you code.

item.title: 12 Terminal
item.description: ABSTRACT The Terminal (also called Command Line or Shell) is a text-based way to talk to your computer.

item.title: 14 GitHub 1
item.description: ABSTRACT If Git is the “Time Machine” on your computer, GitHub is the “Space Station” in the cloud where everyone parks their time machines.

item.title: 15 GitHub 2
item.description: Goal Learn the GitHub workflow and how to use GitHub’s features to collaborate, contribute, and communicate with the rest of the team.

item.title: 07 Configuring Python & Pip
item.description: Goal In this tutorial you will learn to setup your computer to compile and run code in the Python programming language.

item.title: 17 Local $\LaTeX$
item.description: Goal 11_unix 13_terminal Prerequisites Basic collaboration account Installed environment Steps 1.

item.title: 09 Python Basics
item.description: Goal Learn the fundamentals of Python programming: data types, variables, control flow (if/else, loops), and functions.

item.title: 21_CODE_OF_CONDUCT
item.description: Adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1 TL;DR Be kind, be respectful, be professional.
